description:
You are acting as a DevOps engineer who has been asked to investigate a burst of
    “hostname not found” messages.  
    All the “raw” evidence has already been collected for you under
    /home/user/logs and /home/user/resources.

    Goal
    ----
    Analyse the list of hostnames that failed to resolve, determine which ones
    really can be resolved (based on a local override file that was supplied by
    the networking team), and generate two human-readable artefacts.

    Input material
    --------------
    1. /home/user/logs/dns_failures.log  
       • One hostname per line, no leading/trailing whitespace, no blank lines.  

    2. /home/user/resources/hosts_override  
       • Exactly the same format as /etc/hosts:  
         <IPv4-address><single-space><hostname>  
       • Only single spaces are used as separators.  
       • Do NOT alter this file.

    Required working directory
    --------------------------
    • /home/user/dns_debug   (must be created if absent)

    Required output files
    ---------------------
    1. /home/user/dns_debug/resolution_report.txt  
       • UTF-8 plain text.  
       • One logical line per hostname in the SAME order that appears in
         dns_failures.log.  
       • Each logical line consists of exactly **three** fields separated by a
         single TAB character (U+0009):  
           Field-1: the original hostname as seen in dns_failures.log  
           Field-2: literal string “OK” if the name exists in
                    hosts_override, otherwise literal string “FAIL”  
           Field-3: if Field-2 is “OK”, the IPv4 address found in
                    hosts_override; if Field-2 is “FAIL”, a single dash
                    character “-”  
       • No extra whitespace, no trailing TABs, no blank lines at the end.

       Example of the exact shape (spacing shown as ⇥ for clarity):

           some.host.local⇥OK⇥10.0.0.1
           ghost.host.local⇥FAIL⇥-

    2. /home/user/dns_debug/resolution_stats.txt  
       • Exactly TWO lines, each terminated by a single newline (LF, 0x0A).  
       • Line-1: “Total hostnames checked: X” (X replaced by integer count)  
       • Line-2: “Successfully resolved: Y” (Y replaced by integer count)  
       • No extra spaces before/after the colon, no thousands separators.

    Success criteria (what the automated tests verify)
    --------------------------------------------------
    • The directory /home/user/dns_debug exists.
    • Both files above exist and are readable by the current user.
    • resolution_report.txt has precisely one logical line per hostname
      *in the same order* as dns_failures.log and matches the format rules.
    • resolution_stats.txt contains the correct totals that match what
      appears in resolution_report.txt.
    • All field separators are TABs (not spaces, not multiple TABs).
    • No additional files are created inside /home/user/dns_debug.

    What you have to do
    -------------------
    1. Create /home/user/dns_debug.  
    2. Parse /home/user/logs/dns_failures.log.  
    3. For each hostname, look it up **only** in
       /home/user/resources/hosts_override (do NOT consult DNS or /etc/hosts).  
    4. Build resolution_report.txt following the exact tab-separated schema
       described above.  
    5. Compute and write the statistics file.  
    6. Make sure normal file permissions (rw-r--r-- or stricter) are set so
       that the current non-root user can read them.

    Complexity constraints
    ----------------------
    • The whole job should be doable with roughly 5-10 standard shell commands
      (e.g. mkdir, awk/grep, wc, sort, cat, etc.).  Use whatever combination
      you find most efficient.

    Note
    ----
    • Do NOT edit or delete any existing files outside /home/user/dns_debug.
    • Internet access may not be available; rely solely on the supplied files.
    • Make sure the generated files have **exactly** the requested format;
      automated grading will be strict regarding tabs, newlines and spacing.

    Once finished, you’re done—no additional console output is required,
    although you may print progress to stdout while working.
truth:
<!--  Initial data that already exists before the agent starts  -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/logs/dns_failures.log"><![CDATA[
app.internal.local
db.internal.local
cache.internal.local
auth.service
gateway.service
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/resources/hosts_override"><![CDATA[
10.0.0.11 app.internal.local
10.0.0.12 db.internal.local
10.0.0.13 cache.internal.local
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <!--  Files that must be produced by the agent (ground-truth for grading)  -->
    <expected_files>
        <file path="/home/user/dns_debug/resolution_report.txt"><![CDATA[
app.internal.local\tOK\t10.0.0.11
db.internal.local\tOK\t10.0.0.12
cache.internal.local\tOK\t10.0.0.13
auth.service\tFAIL\t-
gateway.service\tFAIL\t-
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/dns_debug/resolution_stats.txt"><![CDATA[
Total hostnames checked: 5
Successfully resolved: 3
]]></file>
    </expected_files>
